messages.content: Big planning day. I need to lock in the full steering install schedule before Friday. We have 3 days and the truck needs to drive by Sunday.

Friday through Sunday gives us 8-9 hours. Let me run through what's on the truck and what needs to happen.

Physical state right now: old steering box has one bolt remaining. Pitman arm is off the old box, hanging on the drag link. PS lines are cut — Borgeson system replaces all of them. Old PS pump still installed, coming out. Interior is stripped, no dash.

All the parts are verified on hand: RedHead 2869-3T box, Borgeson 800328 pump, Borgeson hoses 925117, telescoping shaft 000950, Skyjacker FA400 pitman arm, Sintech clockspring, Forever Sharp 15" wheel with A17 adapter.

For consumables you still need: Valvoline SynPower PS fluid (4 qt), Loctite 242 Blue, anti-seize, new castle nut for the pitman arm, new cotter pin, brake cleaner, degreaser. Roughly $115. Get those Monday through Thursday.

Key decision we settled today: no airbags anywhere in this truck — SRS was completely removed by a previous owner. That eliminates the airbag module, inch-pound torque wrench, capacitor discharge wait, and airbag light test. The Forever Sharp wheel has no airbag provision.

The clockspring decision: install it. The Sintech unit covers future cruise control relocation. Must be centered at 2.5 turns counterclockwise from full clockwise stop before mounting the wheel.

No instrument cluster during the test drive — use your phone for speed, keep runs short.

Deal-breaker torque specs you need posted in the garage: steering box to frame 95 ft-lbs (crisscross 40→70→95 through the PRP brace), pitman arm nut 185 ft-lbs (progressive 50→100→150→185), steering shaft pinch bolts 36 ft-lbs with Loctite 242, PS pump to engine 57 ft-lbs, PS hoses at pump 22 ft-lbs and at box 23 ft-lbs, steering wheel nut 45 ft-lbs.
Wait — the torque spec file on Metis says 225 ft-lbs for the pitman arm. Which is right?

Use 185. The file has a discrepancy — the step-by-step installation procedure in FSM Group 6 says 185 ft-lbs, and that's the service manual spec. The 225 appears to be a carry-over from a different pitman arm application. When specs conflict, the procedure document wins over a spec sheet. I've noted the discrepancy in the torque reference file.

One other thing from today's research: don't go full lock-to-lock for the first 200 miles with the RedHead box. It needs a break-in period.

The A17 adapter is confirmed compatible with the Borgeson 000950 shaft — it's 3/4" 30-spline and the adapter accepts it. The PRP steering box support brace (EL205) is already installed by the previous owner, so you bolt the RedHead straight through it.
Good. What about the SRS wiring harness? I don't want any loose connectors in there.

The SRS wiring harness is dead-ended already — there's no module to connect to and no airbag devices. Cap any open SRS connectors with terminal plugs so they can't short against anything. The clockspring yellow connector routes to where the airbag horn was on the stock wheel; with the Forever Sharp wheel you wire it for horn only through the A17 adapter. That's all.

I've also documented what's been researched and queued for Friday: shopping list with all consumables, the installation procedure checklist in order, parts verification matrix, and the clockspring centering procedure. Everything's staged.

facts: The truck must be driving by Sunday Feb 9 with a work window of Friday Feb 7 through Sunday Feb 9
The work window is estimated at 8-9 hours
SRS and airbags were completely removed by a previous owner — no airbag module exists anywhere in the vehicle
The Forever Sharp steering wheel has no airbag provision
The Sintech Auto clockspring must be centered at 2.5 turns counterclockwise from full clockwise stop before mounting
The pitman arm torque spec is 185 ft-lbs (progressive 50→100→150→185), not 225 ft-lbs from the spec file
The torque spec file discrepancy was noted — FSM Group 6 installation procedure wins over the spec sheet
The RedHead 2869-3T steering box requires a 200-mile break-in period before going full lock-to-lock
The PRP steering box support brace (EL205) is already installed by the previous owner
The A17 adapter is confirmed compatible with the Borgeson 000950 shaft (3/4 inch 30-spline)
Steering box to frame torque is 95 ft-lbs crisscross pattern through the PRP brace
Steering shaft pinch bolts torque to 36 ft-lbs with Loctite 242 on threads
decisions: Install the Sintech Auto clockspring for future cruise control relocation capability
Use 185 ft-lbs for the pitman arm nut, not 225 ft-lbs from the discrepant spec file
contradictions: Torque spec file says 225 ft-lbs for pitman arm; FSM Group 6 installation procedure says 185 ft-lbs — use 185 per service manual procedure

metadata.notes: Sourced from akron/memory/2026-02-02.md — initial steering project planning and deadline confirmation
